lede: A look at September 7 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Buddy Holly and Elizabeth I, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Lokomotiv Yaroslavl plane crash
events.desc: The Lokomotiv Yaroslavl plane crash in Russia kills 43 people, including nearly the entire roster of the Lokomotiv Yaroslavl Kontinental Hockey League team.

events.title: Desmond Tutu
events.desc: Desmond Tutu becomes the first black man to lead the Anglican Diocese of Cape Town.

events.title: Chile
events.desc: Chilean dictator Augusto Pinochet survives an assassination attempt by the FPMR; five of Pinochet's bodyguards are killed.

events.title: C23 tragedy
events.desc: An explosion on board a Maltese patrol boat disposing of illegal fireworks at sea off Gozo kills seven soldiers and policemen.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The German Luftwaffe begins the Blitz, bombing London and other British cities for over 50 consecutive nights.

events.title: Thylacine
events.desc: The last thylacine, a carnivorous marsupial, dies alone in its cage at the Hobart Zoo in Tasmania.

events.title: Interpol
events.desc: The International Criminal Police Organization (INTERPOL) is formed.

events.title: Pedro I of Brazil
events.desc: Dom Pedro I declares Brazil independent from Portugal on the shores of the Ipiranga Brook in São Paulo.

events.title: Third Crusade
events.desc: Third Crusade: Battle of Arsuf: Richard I of England defeats Saladin at Arsuf.

events.title: Pope Alexander III
events.desc: Cardinal Rolando Bandinelli is elected Pope Alexander III, prompting the election of Cardinal Octaviano Monticelli as Antipope Victor IV the same day.

births.name: Baudouin of Belgium
births.line: King of the Belgians from 1951 to 1993

births.name: Walter White (Breaking Bad)
births.line: American fictional protagonist of television series Breaking Bad

births.name: Patrick Jenkin
births.line: Baron Jenkin of Roding, English lawyer and politician, Secretary of State for the Environment

births.name: Todor Zhivkov
births.line: Bulgarian police officer and politician, Head of State of Bulgaria

deaths.text: Walter White (Breaking Bad), American fictional protagonist of television series Breaking Bad
Uziel Gal, German-Israeli colonel and gun designer, designed the Uzi
Edwin McMillan, American physicist and chemist, Nobel Prize laureate
Wilhelm Pieck, German carpenter and politician, President of East Germany
Hannah More, English poet, playwright, and philanthropist
Emperor Xuanzong of Tang (9th century), Chinese emperor
